slice: Engine hardening & leakage battery (blocks all downstream run-workers)

leakage_checks.name: PIT invariance
leakage_checks.asserts: appending a future-dated row leaves every PAST trailing-pctile value and the full build_signals gate stat surface (disp_p80/disp_p33/disp_p67) + resulting gate + size byte-identical

leakage_checks.name: Entry locality
leakage_checks.asserts: open_trade reads ONLY the entry-date EOD chain; a chains.day_chain spy records zero accesses to any trade_date > entry_date

leakage_checks.name: Mark locality
leakage_checks.asserts: day-k mark reads only day-k chain; settlement reads only the expiry chain; re-marking each path prefix leaves the day-k mtm unchanged (causal marking, no look-ahead)

leakage_checks.name: Causal stopping
leakage_checks.asserts: for every ManagementArm (hold/forecast_regate/mechanical_terminal/iv_regate) and every constructed + real-ORATS exit, arm.exit_on returns the identical (k, reason) when the path is truncated at that k; decision uses no row after the exit day

leakage_checks.name: Decision-context purity
leakage_checks.asserts: engine never places target_var (the realized [t,t+h] payoff) into ctx.signal; an EntryContext spy over a real SPY 2020 cell confirms no decision context ever carries target_var; ledger still records target_var (booked from the entry row, schema intact). A runtime assert guard was added in engine._run_one_trade.

bugs_fixed: None — no decision-semantics or LEDGER-schema bugs surfaced; engine/marks/management produced correct, leakage-safe, identity-holding results on real ORATS as written.
changes_non_semantic: engine.py: added a runtime assert guard ('target_var' not in ctx.signal) at EntryContext construction (L2 leakage guard; no semantic change).

chains.py: added @lru_cache(maxsize=8192) to day_chain so repeated leg relocations on the same (ticker, trade_date) reuse one filter instead of re-filtering the ticker-year per leg (perf only; results bit-identical, SPY full-OOS pnl unchanged at -212.26).

strike_and_settlement_validation.method: Recomputed by-|delta| strike selection directly from raw ORATS (ticker=<T>/year=<Y>/data.parquet, delta=call delta, put delta=delta-1) and compared to engine structure.legs; validated held-to-expiry settlement = credit - net intrinsic on the actual expiry chain.

cases.note: OTM at expiry -> settlement == full credit
OTM at expiry -> settlement == full credit
COVID-crash entry; spot crashed below both put strikes -> settlement = credit - width = -max_loss (loss correctly capped at defined risk)

strike_and_settlement_validation.summary: Short-leg |delta| matches SHORT_DELTA=0.20 (engine 0.199-0.207) and wings match WING_DELTA=0.07 (engine 0.065-0.078) on SPY+QQQ across calm and crash expiries; held-to-expiry settlement == credit - net intrinsic on the actual chain in all 3 cases (incl. a capped-loss ITM case).

performance.profile_method: cProfile on a 5-name (SPY,QQQ,IWM,XLK,XLF) h22 iron_condor/hold/none full-OOS run; bottleneck was relocate -> day_chain -> polars filter (~11.9k filter calls).
performance.optimization: memoized chains.day_chain per (ticker, trade_date) via lru_cache (leg relocations on the same day now share one filter).
performance.tractability: A 10-name x multi-structure OOS sweep is tractable: a single 10-name cell is ~2.5s / ~24.5s per 1000 trades; e.g. 5 structures x 4 management x 3 hedge = 60 cells ~ a few minutes.

dry_run.scope: engine restricts to the 10-name CLEAN_CORE universe (no single-name CLI flag); ran the full core universe as built.
dry_run.result: completed: EnsembleTopK h=22 iron_condor__hold__none -> 102 trades, 51 portfolio days, 2.5s cell wallclock
